_skill.name: training_unit_annotation
_skill.description: Per-mode writing rules for the racing-engineer LLM training rows. Defines the critique (post-lap, retrospective) and guide (pre-lap, prescriptive) completions that get drafted per training unit, plus the system-prompt template the trainer uses. Consumed by the Streamlit LLM-pipeline tab (claude_sdk drafter + human review) and by `dataset_builder` when fanning units into chat rows.
when_to_use: Drafting training-unit completions in the Streamlit LLM-pipeline tab.
Briefing a human reviewer on what critique vs guide should sound like.
Building the system prompt for `dataset_builder` Step-2 chat rows.

critique.style:
- 1–3 sentences, ≤ 60 words total. - State what happened in the section, then why it cost time. - Reference at least one concrete label (e.g. "the MS in T3", "the
  offset spike on entry") so the model learns to ground its answer
  in the structured context.
- No fix advice — that's `guide`'s job. Stay descriptive.
avoid: vague filler ("nice lap", "keep working on it")
inventing telemetry numbers the labels don't imply
prescriptive language ("you should", "try to") — belongs to guide

guide.audience: the driver, second person, imperative mood
guide.style:
- 1–3 sentences, ≤ 60 words total. - One actionable instruction per child label (parent label sets
  the section context). If the unit is isolated, give one
  instruction for the whole unit.
- Anchor each instruction to the label (e.g. "On the MS in T3,
  release the brake later before turn-in").
- Present tense, imperative. No reflection on past laps.
avoid: critique language ("you lost time because…") — belongs to critique
generic coaching that ignores the labels

claude_prompt_template:
You are drafting a {mode}-mode completion for a racing-engineer LLM
training dataset. The student model will later be prompted with:

    System:  {mode} user, {labels_text}
    User:    <runtime context>

and is expected to produce a response in the {mode} voice. Your job is
to write that response now, using ONLY the labels as context.

Rules for {mode} mode:
{mode_style}

Avoid:
{mode_avoid}

Output ONLY the completion text. No preamble, no quotes, no markdown,
no labels echoed back. Keep it under 60 words.
